narrative.headline: 2020 Toyota RAV4: engine and electrical lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 621 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is the most reported area at 29%, followed by the electrical system (19%) and the fuel/propulsion system (14%). No deaths are reported. Air bags draw fewer complaints (9%) but the most crashes (8) of any area, so they are worth a close look.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 179 reports (29%), tied to 1 crash and 2 injuries. No fires reported.

issues.sample: I PURCHASED A NEW 2020 RAV4 XLE IN DECEMBER 2020, IT IS NOT EVEN 3 MONTHS OLD.  I PARKED MY RAV4 IN MY DRIVEWAY AFTER GETTING HOME FROM WORK.  THE FOLLOWING MORNING MY CAR HAD A DEAD BATTERY.  I HAD THE BATTERY JUMPED BY TOYOTA ROADSIDE SERVICE.  THE CAR STARTED UP, AND I WAS TOL
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 119 reports (19%), including 1 fire, 1 crash, and 4 injuries. Worth checking carefully given the fire and injury reports.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 89 reports (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. A common complaint area but no serious outcomes on record.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: 58 reports (9%), linked to 2 crashes, 2 fires, and 1 injury. A smaller but recurring complaint area.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 55 reports (9%) but the most crashes of any area: 8 crashes and 4 injuries, no fires. Several reports describe air bags failing to deploy, so test thoroughly.
